page: Amazon Web Services r6a.12xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance powered by the AMD EPYC 7R13 processor. Built on the x86_64 architecture, this instance features 48 virtual CPUs mapped to 24 physical cores with hyperthreading enabled. It operates at a clock speed of 3.6 GHz and includes 384.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. The instance runs on the AWS Nitro hypervisor and does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware.

Benchmark data indicates strong computational and memory performance. The instance achieves top-tier results in stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU tests, as well as the Passmark CPU and memory benchmarks. Cryptographic capabilities are supported by hardware-accelerated instructions like AES, VAES, and SHA_NI, leading to strong performance in Passmark encryption tests. Memory bandwidth tests for uncached large-block reads and writes fall within the average range, while cached small-block operations and Redis SET operations demonstrate stable, mid-range throughput.

The r6a.12xlarge offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-intensive workloads by utilizing AMD EPYC processors, which deliver high resource density compared to alternative architectures. However, the lack of local NVMe storage requires reliance on network-attached storage, which may impact I/O-heavy workloads. With a baseline network bandwidth of 19 Gbps, this instance is well-suited for large-scale databases, in-memory caches, data analytics, and memory-bound enterprise applications.
description: Amazon Web Services r6a.12xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor running at 3.6 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture and the AWS Nitro hypervisor, it delivers 384.0 GB of DDR4 memory with a high ratio of 8.0 GB per core. The instance lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs, relying instead on network-attached storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 19 Gbps. Benchmark results show strong performance in stress-ng CPU tests, Passmark memory evaluations, and cryptographic operations, alongside average memory bandwidth for large-block transfers. This configuration provides qualitative cost efficiency for memory-intensive tasks, making it highly suitable for large-scale databases, in-memory caching, and enterprise applications.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r6a.12xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 48 vCPUs, 384 GB DDR4 RAM, and an AMD EPYC processor, delivering strong CPU and memory benchmark performance for databases.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r6a.12xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 48 vCPUs, 384 GB DDR4 RAM, and AMD EPYC processors for enterprise workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized x86_64 server featuring high memory density and strong multi-core processing for demanding database and enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores via AMD EPYC 7R13 processors
Equipped with 384.0 GB of DDR4 memory at an 8.0 GB per core ratio
Strong benchmark performance in CPU, memory, and cryptographic tests
Features 19 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the AWS Nitro hypervisor
Optimized for in-memory databases, caching layers, and data analytics
